topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The acceptance letter sat on the kitchen counter, crisp and mocking. Liam traced the embossed lettering with a thumb, the paper feeling impossibly heavy. He replayed the final practice session in his mind—the missed timing on the crucial play, the hesitant look in the coach’s eyes when he attempted the advanced maneuver. Every muscle in his jaw tightened as he thought about the other hopefuls, the ones who seemed to possess an innate, effortless talent he simply lacked. He picked up his phone, the screen illuminating the hollow look in his eyes. "Of course," he muttered to the empty room, the sound thin and brittle. He shoved the letter into a drawer, the click of the latch echoing the finality of his disappointment. He spent the rest of the evening staring out the window at the darkened field, the cold seep of the evening air mirroring the chill settling deep in his chest.

[story 2]

"Just try harder next time," the words echoed in his head, a constant, abrasive soundtrack to his quiet apartment. Maya stared at her reflection in the darkened windowpane. Her shoulders slumped, a visible surrender to the weight of her failure. She picked up her worn training journal, the pages filled with meticulously logged reps and frustrating misses. Her fingers trembled slightly as she reread the notes from the tryouts, focusing on the single glaring error she’d made during the agility drill. A sharp intake of breath escaped her lips. She felt a burning sensation behind her eyes, a hot, acidic feeling that made her want to scrub her skin raw. She crumpled the page slowly, deliberately, the sound small but charged with a profound sense of inadequacy.

[story 3]

The team roster was posted on the bulletin board, and Liam’s name wasn't there. He stood in the hallway, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead, making the polished floor seem blindingly sterile. He kept running a hand over his closely cropped hair, a nervous habit he couldn't shake. He watched a group of teammates laughing, their camaraderie a warm, impenetrable wall he couldn't breach. A deep, weary sigh escaped him, a sound heavy with unspoken defeat. He turned away from the board, his gaze fixed on a scuff mark on the wall, his entire posture conveying a profound sense of having fallen short of an invisible benchmark. He felt a strange, heavy numbness spreading through his limbs, as if his energy had simply drained away.
